question.text: A tax audit under Section 44AB is mandatory for derivative transactions when:

answer.text: Turnover exceeds Rs. 10 crore regardless of profit or loss, OR turnover is between Rs. 2-10 crore without presumptive taxation and profits are less than 6% of turnover
Turnover exceeds Rs. 1 crore in any financial year
Any loss is incurred from derivative transactions, regardless of turnover
Tax audit is never mandatory for derivative transactions

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Tax audit is mandatory under Section 44AB if: (1) turnover > Rs. 10 Cr (regardless of profit/loss), or (2) turnover Rs. 2-10 Cr, not using presumptive taxation, and profits < 6% of turnover. Exception: Rs. 2-10 Cr with >95% digital transactions may be exempt. **The Trap:** Rs. 10 Cr is the absolute threshold. Between Rs. 2-10 Cr, it depends on profitability and digital transaction percentage. **Cross-Exam Context:** Tax audit thresholds are regulatory facts in Series VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** > Rs. 10 Cr = mandatory audit. Rs. 2-10 Cr = audit if profits < 6% (without presumptive). ≤ Rs. 2 Cr = use Section 44AD.
